description: Topic catalog for the MCP-grounded UE5 SFT pipeline. Each topic is a prompt seed for one data_type. Topics are organized so that every claim in the generated example can be grounded in MCP-observable state from the live IntroToUE project.

concept_qa.description: Multi-turn Q&A about a UE5 concept, with concrete examples drawn from the live project.

topics.topic: Collision channels and the Projectile channel
topics.description: Why custom channels exist, how they're declared, how profiles reference them.

topics.topic: GameMode vs PlayerController vs Pawn in this project
topics.description: How the three roles split responsibility in a first-person template.

topics.topic: Lumen and Substrate choices in DefaultEngine.ini
topics.description: Why Lumen is on, Substrate is off, and what that implies for materials.

topics.topic: Virtual Shadow Maps in a Lumen-enabled level
topics.description: How VSM interacts with Lumen and Nanite in this project's graphics config.

topics.topic: Input mapping contexts (IMC_Default vs IMC_MouseLook)
topics.description: Why two IMCs and what each binds, based on this project's Input/ folder.

topics.topic: SaveGame subsystem and BP_SaveData
topics.description: How the project's SaveData class is intended to be used.

topics.topic: Plugin loading order and what gets enabled in IntroToUE
topics.description: ModelingToolsEditorMode, Landmass, InEditorDocumentation, AIAssistant, MCPClientToolset, ModelContextProtocol.

topics.topic: World Partition vs single-level in Lvl_IntroRoom
topics.description: Whether this level uses World Partition, how to tell, and what changes if you switch.

topics.topic: First Person template: BP_FirstPersonCharacter and its components
topics.description: Walk through what this character is composed of and how it drives the player.

topics.topic: Asset naming conventions in this project (BP_, M_, MI_, NS_, ...)
topics.description: Why the prefixes exist and how they help with Content Browser searching.

topics.topic: r.AllowStaticLighting=False and what that forces on the project
topics.description: Why dynamic-only lighting means Lumen is non-optional, and what lighting workflow shifts.

topics.topic: Trigger volumes in DemoTemplate (BP_DoorTrigger, BP_DoorFrame_Unlockable)
topics.description: How the door-unlock pattern is set up across two BPs that pair together.

tool_use.description: Real MCP tool-call chains. User asks, assistant calls tools, observes, optionally retries. Includes error-recovery examples driven by the live MCP's natural error states.

topics.topic: Inventorying actors by class
topics.description: List every actor of a given class, summarize counts.

topics.topic: Getting details of a specific actor
topics.description: Find an actor by name pattern, fetch its full transform.

topics.topic: Discovering the available MCP toolsets
topics.description: List toolsets, describe each, summarize capabilities for the assistant.

topics.topic: Reading the project context the AI Assistant sees
topics.description: Pull GetProjectContext, parse the engine / project / user fields.

topics.topic: Locating the player character and its game mode
topics.description: Find the BP_FirstPersonGameMode and BP_FirstPersonCharacter actors.

topics.topic: Error recovery: GetActorDetails on a non-existent actor
topics.description: Tool returns an error; the assistant recognizes, lists actors, and tries a real name.

topics.topic: Error recovery: execute_console_command typo (blocked vs wrong)
topics.description: A typo'd console command is rejected by the safety allow-list. The assistant recognizes and uses a working alternative.

topics.topic: Saving the level after a series of inspection calls
topics.description: User asks for a 'no-op' save; the assistant calls save_current_level to confirm persistence.

topics.topic: Identifying the most numerous actor class in the level
topics.description: ListActors → group by class → report top N.

scene_understanding.description: Read-and-explain the level. Detect issues, identify game-mode actors, summarize the scene.

topics.topic: Identify the level's purpose from its actor mix
topics.description: From the 391 actors, infer the level is an intro room for the FirstPerson template.

topics.topic: Detect potentially missing prerequisites (PlayerStart, GameMode)
topics.description: Confirm both BP_FirstPersonGameMode and a PlayerStart exist; flag if not.

topics.topic: Find actor class outliers (single-instance classes)
topics.description: Which classes have only one instance? Often the level's unique actors (game mode, lighting, post-process).

topics.topic: Map class distribution to level complexity
topics.description: Top-N actor classes, what they imply about the scene's complexity.

topics.topic: Identify static vs dynamic lighting actors by class name
topics.description: DirectionalLight, PointLight, SpotLight, RectLight — and whether any are missing in a Lumen-enabled level.

topics.topic: Sanity-check the level: actors at origin (0,0,0)
topics.description: Find any actor whose location is exactly (0,0,0) — could indicate a forgotten default placement.

console_diagnosis.description: Console commands and their meaning in the context of this scene. Read-mostly with auto-restored overrides.

topics.topic: What 'stat fps' shows in this scene
topics.description: Predict the output structure; the scene has 391 actors so GPU work is moderate.

topics.topic: What 'show collision' reveals
topics.description: Toggles the debug collision overlay; pair with a ListActors call to know what to expect.

topics.topic: r.ScreenPercentage trade-offs in a Lumen level
topics.description: What halving ScreenPercentage does to a Lumen-enabled scene; pair with actor count to estimate cost.

topics.topic: obj list class=Light for asset audit
topics.description: List every loaded Light asset to see what's compiled in.

topics.topic: MemReport interpretation for this level
topics.description: What a MemReport dump contains and how to read the top-N section.

topics.topic: ke * for kismet event dump on this scene
topics.description: Walk through what kismet events are live.

topics.topic: r.Lumen.DiffuseIndirect.Allow toggle and its visual effect
topics.description: Predict what the level looks like with indirect GI disabled; restore after.
